name: Port Said Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: Port Said's heroism in the face of the 1956 Anglo-French invasion; the 'red carpet' epithet of the city
the lighthouse and other emblems of Port Said as a Mediterranean port at the entrance of the Suez Canal

description: A red field charged in the centre with the yellow emblem of Port Said Governorate.
history: The flag of Port Said Governorate displays its emblem in yellow on a red field commemorating the city's resistance during the 1956 Suez Crisis. It was adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s.

features.symbolism: the resistance and resilience of Port Said, recognised by Egypt with the title of 'city of valour' for its role in the 1956 Suez Crisis
the emblem of Port Said, including a lighthouse and maritime motifs, recalling the city's role at the northern entrance of the Suez Canal
